People: 1635: Came up to a Honolulu brig flying signal of distress. Shortened & furled all sail, stopped engines and sent a boat aboard in charge of W.P. Ray Master U.S.N. Ascertained her name to be the Violet 30 hours out from San Francisco bound to Honolulu, and that she was on fire and needed help.

People: 1800-2000: Working party still at work at the Brigantine Violet endeavouring to extinguish fire in her. Came on board from the Violet 11 passengers. 5 Europeans, and 6 Chinamen. Recd United States Mail from Violet. Also charts, books and chronometers

2000-2400: Sent 1st Cutter to land Captain's Clerk. Sent with dispatches to brig's owners in San Francisco. During first part of watch boats returned from brig Violet all possible aid having been rendered
